executive_summary: 8th consecutive poll of PRED-105 silent-ignore continuation. Only new data point of any weight: the ~5-day manual-commit drought threshold previously flagged as imminent has now been CROSSED — last manual commit a57e731 is ~130.1h (5.42d) old, with 12 consecutive auto commits since. This is still SUBSTANTIVE-class per our pre-locked rubric, not CRITICAL: the rubric fires response only on drought-break or goalpost-move, not on drought-continuation. Pattern is otherwise identical to the 106h continuation analyzed earlier — FAIL-009 correctly logs the silent-ignore and predictions.json our_verdict=silent_ignore is correct. Filing one LOW-priority cosmetic issue-proposal this poll: FAIL-009.outcome text still quotes "~94h (3.92d)" / "6 consecutive polls" / "5 polls byte-identical", which has now drifted materially (actual: 118h / 8 polls / 7 polls byte-identical, with 5-day drought crossed). Prior run deliberately left this as stale-text-cosmetic-not-logged; at 118h it should at least be visible to the decider.
kernel_of_truth: The charitable defense for ~5.4 days of silence after a 14-day cucumber-growth window closes: a seedling trial that starts at registration and runs 14 days to close 2026-04-18 reasonably needs post-run measurement, data entry, and write-up — a few extra days are not by themselves prima-facie bad faith, and a solo author can legitimately be offline, traveling, or batching a larger commit. That is fair. What resists the charitable reading is the combination: (a) predictions.html is byte-identical across 7 polls, not stashed-and-rebased, so no edit-in-progress is visible; (b) the auto-pipeline has run 12 times in the last ~12h and keeps the 94.5% registry number unchanged without treating PRED-105 as a falsification, so the denominator exclusion survives each pipeline tick — which is exactly the silent-ignore pattern; (c) the 5-day window for adjudication is now past, not approaching; (d) last comparable PRED-072 silent-ignore is already at 12 days and counting, which shows this is not a one-off delay but a class pattern. The kernel of legitimacy for a given delay does not compose across multiple simultaneous open-window non-adjudications.

counter_considerations: Opposing considerations per user preference: (1) Author-absence / batching hypothesis. 5.4d of commit silence for a solo author is inside the range of ordinary travel, illness, or large-batch preparation. Absence of commits is not the same as absence of work. Against this: predictions.html is byte-identical across 7 polls — any local editing would eventually surface as a push that flips the byte-hash, and no such flip is present. (2) Experimental-completion hypothesis. A cucumber-seedling trial that ends 2026-04-18 legitimately needs a few days of measurement, photo, and write-up; silent-as-of-5-days is not absurd. Against this: the pre-registration SHA of 2026-04-04 already budgets the whole 14-day growth interval; and this is now two co-running silent-ignores (PRED-072 at ~12d, PRED-105 at ~4.92d), so the one-off-delay defense does not extend across the class. (3) Pipeline-design defense (repeat from 106h analysis). Auto-pipeline output is input-driven; absence of a reported outcome simply produces no refined-bucket increment. That is mechanically correct — and is exactly the shape of silent-ignore; the defense describes the behavior rather than refuting it. (4) Our own tracking could be over-indexing on a poll cadence that compounds apparent severity. Fair point — "8 consecutive polls" sounds larger than "4.92 days" and is partially artefact of how often the poller runs. We should be careful in prose not to present poll count as independent evidence of duration.
gotchas: Do not mark FAIL-009 as reconciled-again this run — reconciled_at 2026-04-20 stands. The cosmetic refresh is an outcome-text edit, not a reconciliation event.

The 5-day drought THRESHOLD was flagged as CRITICAL-adjacent by the poller notes but our pre-locked rubric classifies drought-CROSSING as SUBSTANTIVE (not CRITICAL). Only drought-BREAKING (first manual commit after threshold) triggers substantive analyst action (canary + parameter diff). Do not over-escalate the crossing itself.

daily_review.json did NOT regenerate this window. The next regen is expected ~2026-04-23T07:06Z UTC (hours after this analyst run) — if registry_ratio_pct moves or refined increments at that regen, it is a distinct new signal worth tracking on next poll.
Cosmetic issue-proposal is LOW severity; the decider may choose to batch it with other FAIL-009 edits when PRED-105 is actually adjudicated.
Do not conflate PRED-105 silent-ignore with PRED-072 silent-ignore. Different windows, different evidence, separate FAIL-010 entry; avoid pooled counting that could be challenged as bundling.
tldr: 8th consecutive poll of PRED-105 silent-ignore (~4.92d past close). 5-day manual-commit drought crossed this poll (5.42d since last manual commit) — the crossing itself is SUBSTANTIVE, not CRITICAL; only a drought-break triggers active response. Auto-pipeline ran 12x and left the dome 94.5% accuracy number unchanged. One LOW-priority cosmetic issue-proposal filed to refresh FAIL-009.outcome text from stale 94h/6-polls counters to current 118h/8-polls; no structural change, no expansion, no canary signal.
